notes: QA #36 â€” Site confirmed live (HTTP 200). Screenshots attempted via Puppeteer; system thread limits prevented browser launch, referencing QA #29 screenshots as visual baseline. Thorough code review of App.jsx, Office.jsx, Office.css, App.css, and utils/. 10 of 12 issues from Review #35 remain unresolved (one confirmed fix: ActivityLog filtering now done at call site; one new improvement: activity filter is now functional). Five new issues discovered: `w.started` undefined bug in stuck-worker detection, stats-bar 7-card grid overflow, completionToast missing `role=alert`, Office.css characters missing prefers-reduced-motion, and activity-filter-bar using wrong ARIA radio interaction pattern. Accessibility score held at 2 â€” multiple keyboard-only blockers remain across tags, Office chars, and the stuck-worker stat card. Performance and code quality marginally improve but the `activeWorkers` non-memoization issue, duplicate `getRelativeTime`, and divergent color maps are all still present.

suggestions.title: Keyboard shortcut sets ##office double-hash in URL
suggestions.description: In App.jsx (lines 604â€“605), the keyboard handler does `window.location.hash = '#office'` and `window.location.hash = '#dashboard'`. Assigning a string that already includes '#' to location.hash results in a double-hash (##office) in the URL. The hashchange listener then reads `window.location.hash.slice(1)` â†’ '#office', which fails the equality check against 'office', so browser back/forward does not restore the active tab. Fix: change both keyboard shortcut assignments to use plain strings without '#' prefix â€” matching the tab-button click handlers on lines 887 and 900 which correctly use `window.location.hash = 'office'`.

suggestions.title: ProgressBar milestone Set never resets â€” flash fires once forever
suggestions.description: In the ProgressBar component (App.jsx ~line 173), `milestonesRef` is `useRef(new Set())`. Milestones (25, 50, 75, 100) are added to the Set when hit but the Set is never cleared. If a worker gets a new task (progress resets to 0), the milestone flashes will never fire again because the Set still contains all previously-seen milestones. Fix: in the useEffect, check if the new progress is lower than the minimum tracked milestone and clear the Set when a task resets (e.g., when `updatedAt` changes and `progress < 25`). Alternatively, key the Set off `updatedAt` using a ref that resets when `updatedAt` changes.

suggestions.title: task-tag <span> elements are mouse-only â€” keyboard users cannot filter
suggestions.description: Task-tag spans in WorkerCard (App.jsx lines 278â€“290) have `onClick` and a `cursor: pointer` style but no `tabIndex`, no `role="button"`, and no `onKeyDown`/`onKeyPress` handler. Keyboard-only users and screen reader users cannot activate tag filters. Fix: add `tabIndex={0}`, `role="button"`, and an `onKeyDown` handler that calls `onTagClick` on Enter/Space. Also add a `focus-visible` outline style in App.css for `.task-tag`.

suggestions.title: Clickable Character divs in Office.jsx lack role=button
suggestions.description: The Character component in Office.jsx (line 374) renders a `<div>` with `tabIndex={0}` and an `onClick` handler when `onWorkerClick` is provided. Without `role="button"`, screen readers announce it as a generic group element and do not hint that it is interactive. Additionally, there is no `onKeyDown` handler for Enter/Space activation. Fix: when `handleClick` is defined, add `role="button"` to the div and an `onKeyDown` that calls `handleClick` on Enter/Space.

suggestions.title: w.started is undefined â€” stuck-worker detection never triggers
suggestions.description: In StatsBar (App.jsx line 345), `stuckWorkers` filters by `w.started` but worker objects have `updated_at`, not `started`. `new Date(undefined).getTime()` returns NaN, making the `> 7_200_000` comparison always false â€” so the Stuck stat card never appears. Line 400 then calls `formatDuration(stuckWorkers[0].started)` which returns `null` (empty duration label). Fix: replace `w.started` with `w.updated_at` throughout this logic (or whichever field correctly represents when the task began).

suggestions.title: ETA calculation uses updated_at instead of task start â€” produces nonsense
suggestions.description: ProgressBar (App.jsx line 190): `workingMs = Date.now() - new Date(updatedAt)` where `updatedAt` is the last progress update time, not when the task started. If progress is static for hours (e.g., stuck at 60%), `workingMins` grows but `progress` stays fixed, causing the estimated minutes remaining to grow without bound rather than converge. Fix: either remove the ETA display entirely, or require a `startedAt` field from the worker data to compute a meaningful velocity-based estimate.

suggestions.title: activeWorkers not memoized â€” 5+ useEffect hooks re-run on every render
suggestions.description: In App.jsx (line 637), `activeWorkers` is computed with a plain `.filter()` call, creating a new array reference on every render. Five downstream `useEffect` hooks (lines 644, 707, 728, 746, 770+) and multiple child components receive this as a prop, causing unnecessary re-renders and effect re-executions even when worker data hasn't changed. Fix: wrap in `useMemo`: `const activeWorkers = useMemo(() => allWorkers.filter(w => w.status === 'working' || w.status === 'idle' || w.status === 'error'), [allWorkers])`.

suggestions.title: Stuck-worker stat card clickable but not keyboard-accessible
suggestions.description: The Stuck stat card in StatsBar (App.jsx lines 389â€“404) has an `onClick` handler that scrolls to the stuck worker card, but no `tabIndex`, no `role`, and no `onKeyDown` handler. Keyboard users cannot activate it. Fix: add `tabIndex={0}`, `role="button"`, `aria-label` (e.g., `Scroll to stuck worker: ${stuckWorkers[0].name}`), and an `onKeyDown` handler for Enter/Space.

suggestions.title: completionToast disappears instantly â€” no exit animation
suggestions.description: The completion toast (App.jsx lines 807â€“831) has a bounce-in animation (`badge-pop`) but vanishes abruptly when `setTimeout(() => setCompletionToast(null), 3000)` fires. There's no fade-out or slide-out. Fix: use a CSS class-based approach â€” render the toast with an `is-exiting` class added ~300ms before removal, apply a fade/slide-out keyframe to that class, then remove after the animation completes.

suggestions.title: Activity filter bar ARIA radio pattern is broken â€” no arrow-key navigation
suggestions.description: The filter bar (App.jsx lines 988â€“1004) uses `role="radiogroup"` on the container and `role="radio"` / `aria-checked` on buttons, but the ARIA radio group pattern requires arrow keys to move focus between options (not Tab). Currently Tab visits each button individually â€” wrong for a radio group. There's also no `name` attribute on the pseudo-radios. Fix: implement roving tabindex (only active button gets tabIndex=0, others get -1) and add `onKeyDown` arrow navigation within the group â€” or switch to actual `<input type="radio">` elements styled to match the current pill look.

suggestions.title: completionToast uses aria-live=polite â€” should be role=alert for immediate announcement
suggestions.description: The completion toast (App.jsx line 809) uses `aria-live="polite"`, which delays screen reader announcements until the current interaction is idle. For a transient notification celebrating task completion, this means the user may miss it entirely. Fix: change to `role="alert"` (which implies `aria-live="assertive"`) so screen readers announce it immediately on insertion.

suggestions.title: Stats bar 5-column grid breaks with 7 cards (error + stuck both visible)
suggestions.description: The `.stats-bar` CSS (App.css line 118) defines `grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 1fr)` for the default 5 cards (Total, Active, Idle, Sync, Vibe). When both the error card AND the stuck card appear simultaneously, there are 7 cards total, causing the 7th to be narrower or to overflow unexpectedly on mobile breakpoints. Fix: change to `repeat(auto-fill, minmax(130px, 1fr))` or explicitly handle 6 and 7-card layouts in CSS.

suggestions.title: WORKER_ROLE_COLORS in App.jsx keyed by worker name â€” diverges from ROLE_COLORS keyed by role
suggestions.description: App.jsx (line 14) defines `WORKER_ROLE_COLORS` as `{ Maya: '#a855f7', Carlos: '#3b82f6', ... }` â€” keyed by name. `ROLE_COLORS` in constants.js is keyed by role string (e.g., `'Frontend Engineer': '#a78bfa'`). These two maps assign different colors for the same workers and are used in different places (confetti coloring vs card accent). The divergence creates visual inconsistency: confetti for Maya uses `#a855f7` but her card uses `#a78bfa`. Fix: either derive `WORKER_ROLE_COLORS` from `ROLE_COLORS` via the worker's role field, or consolidate to a single source of truth.

suggestions.title: getRelativeTime duplicated â€” defined in App.jsx and inlined in Office.jsx ghost bubble
suggestions.description: App.jsx defines `getRelativeTime` at line 34. Office.jsx (lines 394â€“403) inlines an identical relative-time calculation inside the ghost bubble IIFE rather than importing the shared function. Fix: move `getRelativeTime` to `utils/time.js` (or similar), export it, and import it in both App.jsx and Office.jsx.

suggestions.title: Office.css character animations missing prefers-reduced-motion guard
suggestions.description: App.css (line 886) has a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block that disables card/shimmer animations, but Office.css has no equivalent. Animations defined in Office.css (enter-office, char-bob, wander-1 through wander-4, char-glitch, fade-in-char, deep-work-pulse) will still run for users who have requested reduced motion. Fix: add a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block to Office.css that sets `animation: none !important` on `.char`, `.char--working`, `.char--idle`, `.char--glitch`, and related elements.

suggestions.title: Office floor has no 360px breakpoint â€” characters and text become illegible
suggestions.description: The smallest CSS breakpoint in App.css is 420px. At 360px viewports (common on budget Android devices), the office floor (900px max-width, aspect-ratio 4/3) scales down to ~340px wide and ~255px tall. SVG avatars at 36â€“44px become pinhead-sized, desk nameplates are unreadable, and the whiteboard text overflows. Fix: add a `@media (max-width: 380px)` block in Office.css that increases font sizes for `.office-sign`, `.desk__nameplate`, and `.mgr-desk__nameplate`, and reduces avatar sizes via a CSS custom property.

suggestions.title: WorkerCard outer div has tabIndex=0 with role=article â€” creates confusing double tab stop
suggestions.description: WorkerCard (App.jsx line 250) renders a `<div tabIndex={0} role="article">` wrapper. The article itself is not interactive â€” the â„ąď¸Ź flip button inside it handles the main interaction. Having the outer container in the tab order creates a meaningless focus stop that announces as 'article' with no obvious action. Fix: remove `tabIndex={0}` from the outer wrapper (it will still receive focus via keyboard via the inner buttons). If the intent is to allow scrollIntoView on focus, use programmatic `.focus()` on the inner button instead.
